entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop grown in semi-arid tropical areas.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICAL AREAS
entities.description: Regions characterized by low and irregular rainfall and high temperatures.

entities.name: SOWING DENSITY
entities.description: The number of plants sown per unit area, tested at high and low levels.

entities.name: HIGH DENSITY
entities.description: A sowing density of 22 plants per square meter.

entities.name: LOW DENSITY
entities.description: A sowing density of 11 plants per square meter.

entities.name: RAINFED CONDITIONS
entities.description: Agricultural conditions where water is supplied only by precipitation.

entities.name: FIELD EXPERIMENTS
entities.description: Research conducted in actual field conditions to test crop response.

entities.name: LYSIMETER EXPERIMENTS
entities.description: Experiments using lysimeters to precisely measure water use and efficiency.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The weight of the grain produced per unit area.

entities.name: BIOMASS YIELD
entities.description: The total biological mass produced by the plant per unit area.

entities.name: WATER USE
entities.description: The total amount of water consumed by the crop.

entities.name: WATER USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The ratio of biomass or grain yield to the amount of water used.

entities.name: TILLERING CAPACITY
entities.description: The ability of the plant to produce side shoots or tillers.

entities.name: LEAF AREA INDEX
entities.description: The ratio of total upper leaf surface of a crop to the surface area of the land.

entities.name: DRY SEASON
entities.description: The period of the year with minimal rainfall.

entities.name: WET SEASON
entities.description: The period of the year with maximum rainfall.

entities.name: BREEDING
entities.description: The science of changing the traits of plants to produce desired characteristics.

entities.name: ELITE CULTIVARS
entities.description: High-performing sorghum varieties selected for testing.

relationships.description: Sorghum is typically sown in semi-arid tropical areas
Increasing sowing density increases grain yield
Increasing sowing density increases biomass yield
Increasing sowing density increases water use
Increasing sowing density affects water use efficiency
High density significantly increases biomass yield
High density significantly increases grain yield
High density increases water use by 38%
High density increases water use efficiency by 17%
Tight positive link between biomass response to density and WUE increase in dry season
Negative link between biomass response to density and WUE increase in wet season
Leaf area index increase explains the response to high density
No link found between tillering capacity and response to density
Context for the tight positive link between biomass response and WUE
Context for the negative link between biomass response and WUE

Developing cultivars adapted to high sowing density
